summary: Backend-only testing of JobPortal 20-80. 30 pytest tests across auth, employee/employer profiles, jobs CRUD, AI matching (Claude Sonnet 4.5), applications, role-based access control, and admin management. ALL 30 passed including the LLM-backed /api/employee/jobs/suggested endpoint.

minor.issue: When percentage_min>percentage_max the manual 400 check is reachable only after Pydantic ge/le validation passes. With percentage_min=80, percentage_max=40 the server returns 400 (not 422) because both individual values pass Pydantic constraints. Acceptable, but inconsistent with how min>max gets handled in /employee/profile.
Returns 200 instead of more conventional 201 Created. Functionally fine.
Uses query param `blocked` (bool) instead of JSON body. Works but unusual for a POST.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 462 lines and packs auth, employee, employer, jobs, applications, admin, updates into a single _mount_routes() closure — should be split into routers/ submodules for maintainability.
CORS_ORIGINS='*' combined with allow_credentials=True is invalid per CORS spec; browsers will reject credentialed requests. Set explicit origins.
Email is lowercased on insert but EmailStr keeps the original case in JWT/responses on /register path is fine; verified responses return lowercased email — consistent.
No brute-force / rate limiting on /api/auth/login (auth playbook recommends lockout after 5 failures).

/api/employee/jobs/suggested calls compute_match for every overlapping job in parallel via asyncio.gather — could be expensive (token cost + latency) for large job tables. Consider caching or top-K filtering before LLM call.
matching.py: JSON extraction relies on regex; if Claude returns multiple JSON blocks the regex .* is greedy and may grab too much. Works in practice but fragile.
Admin block endpoint uses POST with query param `?blocked=true` — should be a JSON body or a PATCH for clarity.
No password complexity validation beyond min_length=6.
JWT secret defaulting via os.environ['JWT_SECRET'] is fine but no rotation / refresh-token mechanism — only 7-day access tokens.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@jobportal.ch / Admin123! (auto-seeded, idempotent password reset confirmed). Test users created/deleted per run with prefix test_emp_/test_emr_.
seed_data_creation: Per-run unique employee + employer users created and cleaned up at end of suite (TestZZCleanup). Admin update lifecycle creates+deletes a TEST update.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully green. backend_test.py is a complete regression suite (30 tests, ~11s). It registers fresh test users each run, exercises Claude Sonnet 4.5 via /api/employee/jobs/suggested (real LLM call, ~5s), and self-cleans. When testing the frontend, you can rely on backend stability. Admin seeding is idempotent and resets the password to env value on each startup. JWT auth is via `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header only (no cookies used).
rca of the issue: N/A — no functional bugs. Three initial test failures were caused by my tests asserting on mixed-case TEST_ email prefix while the backend correctly normalises emails to lowercase. Fixed by using lowercase email prefix in tests.
